narrative:
A front moves through this evening with only spotty light showers, but the bigger story is the last batch of cold air arriving tonight and lingering through Friday morning. Gusty winds develop later tonight into Thursday with freezing temperatures at the higher elevations Thursday morning. Then temperatures tumble again Thursday night into early Friday with frost and freezing temperatures for much of the mountains. After a chilly Friday morning, much warmer weather is on the way late Friday through the weekend. It's a quick flip to summer.

This week's Monday Message covers a range of topics, from record high temperatures to pin-pointing where the best rainfall will be. Thanks to our subscribers!

Stray Showers; Colder Tonight and Thursday

Last Updated Wed May 13 at 3:59PM by Alan Simmons

A front moves through this evening with only spotty light showers, but the bigger story is the last batch of cold air arriving tonight and lingering through Friday morning. Gusty winds develop later tonight into Thursday with freezing temperatures at the higher elevations Thursday morning. Then temperatures tumble again Thursday night into early Friday with frost and freezing temperatures for much of the mountains. After a chilly Friday morning, much warmer weather is on the way late Friday through the weekend. It's a quick flip to summer.

This week's Monday Message covers a range of topics, from record high temperatures to pin-pointing where the best rainfall will be. Thanks to our subscribers!

Forecast Discussion

A trough moving through the eastern U.S. today and Thursday pushes a front across the Southern Appalachians this evening. The precipitation with this system is light and spotty, with a bit more emphasis on the western edge of the Appalachians. Stray light rain may linger into tonight, especially west, and a curiosity-level snow flurry is possible at the highest elevations.

The core of a strong upper-level system moves from Wisconsin today to Pennsylvania on Thursday and to the New England coast Friday. It exits quickly northeast Friday and Saturday. The effect on us is a sharply colder air mass arriving tonight and lingering through Thursday into Friday morning. Expect gusty winds later tonight into Thursday along with freezing conditions at the higher elevations Thursday morning. Then temperatures tumble even farther Thursday night into early Friday with freezing temperatures possible across much of the mountains. The "Mother's Day Rule" for frost did not work this year.

Surface high pressure builds in quickly Friday. It moves into the western Atlantic Saturday but extends back into the Southeast through the rest of this 7-day forecast.

Weak trailing energy passes to our north Saturday and could spark a stray shower, but warmer temperatures will be the main thing you notice.

Then Sunday through Tuesday, we are on the western edge of high pressure centered in the Atlantic. A warm, moist southerly flow flips our switch to summertime. Temperatures turn markedly warmer. With that summer pattern comes the potential for renegade afternoon and early evening showers and thundershowers each day.

Next Wednesday through Friday, a front approaches and could stall nearby. Maybe that would bring some drought relief, but I'll believe it when I see it.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Monday Message for May 11, 2026

This week's Monday Message covers a range of topics, from record high temperatures to pin-pointing where the best rainfall will be. Thanks to our subscribers!

May 11, 2026
May Chill Continues

It was another chilly start to the day, with areas of valley frost in the mountains. We have a similar bout of chill Friday morning, before we quickly toggle into a summertime weather pattern over the upcoming weekend.

May 12, 2026
WWR? The Parkway Foundation

There’s nothing quite like the Blue Ridge Parkway, and the Parkway Foundation plays a huge role in keeping it beautiful for everyone to enjoy. Join us as we sit down with the Foundation’s CEO, Carolyn, to talk about the programs and projects making a difference along the Parkway — and how you can help preserve the place we all love. #ExploreWNC
